sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Watch Rollins CFO’s Barclays conference talk live Feb. 18
feed.summary: Rollins, Inc. (NYSE: ROL) announced that its Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, Kenneth Krause, will present at the Barclays 43rd Annual Industrial Select Conference on February 18, 2026. The presentation will be webcast live from 1:50 p.m. to 2:20 p.m. ET and a replay will be available for 180 days on the company's investor page. Rollins is a global consumer and commercial services company specializing in pest control.

feed.title: Apple to stream video podcasts on its platforms
feed.summary: Apple has announced that video podcasting will now be available on its platforms, entering a space already occupied by YouTube, Netflix, and Spotify. This move positions Apple to compete directly with these platforms by offering users the ability to both listen to and watch podcasts, with the flexibility to switch between formats. The announcement follows other recent podcast-related deals in the industry, indicating a growing trend in video podcast content.

feed.title: Teledyne FLIR OEM Expands Neutrino ISR Series with Longest-Range Model
feed.summary: Teledyne FLIR OEM has expanded its Neutrino ISR Series with the launch of the Neutrino SX8 ISR 50-1000, which is their longest-range model to date. This high-performance thermal imaging solution features a 20x continuous zoom and is designed for demanding long-range intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) applications such as border security and counter-UAS missions. It offers significant detection, recognition, and identification ranges and incorporates advanced features like AI readiness and seamless integration capabilities.

feed.title: Rollins to Present at Upcoming Investor Conference
feed.summary: Rollins, Inc. announced that its Executive Vice President and CFO, Kenneth Krause, will present at the Barclays 43rd Annual Industrial Select Conference. The presentation is scheduled for Wednesday, February 18, from 1:50 p.m. to 2:20 p.m. E.T. and will be webcast live on the company's investor relations website.

feed.title: Public Storage to relocate to North Texas
feed.summary: Public Storage, a company that has been based in Southern California for over 50 years, is reportedly relocating its operations to North Texas. The article, while brief, highlights this significant move for the company.

feed.title: Xcel Energy may cut power to 15,000 in Texas Panhandle amid wildfire risk Tuesday
feed.summary: Xcel Energy plans to implement a "Public Safety Power Shut Off" (PSPS) starting Tuesday morning, potentially affecting 15,000 people across 16 counties in the Texas Panhandle. This measure is a proactive effort to reduce wildfire risk due to high winds and dry conditions. Power restoration after such an event could take several hours to multiple days as crews need to check for damage before re-energizing lines.

feed.title: Does Illinois Tool Works’ (ITW) Rising Payout and Buybacks Redefine Its Capital Allocation Priorities?
feed.summary: Illinois Tool Works (ITW) reported strong Q4 2025 results with increased sales and net income, alongside significant share repurchases and a declared dividend, reinforcing its commitment to returning capital to shareholders. The company's investment narrative focuses on steady cash generation, disciplined capital returns, and modest growth, which is supported by its 2026 revenue and EPS guidance. While the company's dividend and buyback strategies are reassuring, investors should be mindful of potential segment weaknesses that could challenge profit targets.

feed.title: BGSF, Inc. Announces Timing of Fiscal 2025 Third Quarter Results and Earnings Conference Call
feed.summary: BGSF, Inc. (NYSE:BGSF) is set to release its fiscal 2025 third quarter results on Wednesday, November 5, 2025, after market close. Following the release, management will host an earnings conference call and webcast on Thursday, November 6, 2025, at 9:00 am ET. Call and replay details have been provided for interested participants and investors.

feed.title: Hardware is King: Applied Materials Defies AI Software Slump with Massive Breakout
feed.summary: Applied Materials (NASDAQ: AMAT) experienced a significant breakout, with its shares surging over 8.1% to a record $354.91, amidst a broader "SaaSpocalypse" affecting AI software companies. This surge was driven by strong Q1 fiscal earnings, which beat analyst expectations, and positive forward-looking guidance, including a projected 20% growth for the semiconductor equipment business in 2026. The company also resolved a long-standing regulatory risk with a $252.5 million settlement, marking a shift in market sentiment towards hardware infrastructure providers over AI software services due to increasing ROI skepticism and automation concerns.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries and Renesas Expand Strategic Collaboration to Enhance Semiconductor Manufacturing and Supply Chain Resilience
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries and Renesas Electronics have announced an expanded multi-billion-dollar strategic partnership to strengthen semiconductor supply chains for automotive and IoT applications. This collaboration will give Renesas access to GlobalFoundries' advanced technology platforms, with manufacturing planned across the U.S., Germany, Singapore, and potential integration into Renesas' Japanese facilities. The initiative aims to support the growing demand for semiconductors in advanced automotive technologies and enhance domestic production for economic and national security.

feed.title: Every New Car Offering 0% Financing in February 2026
feed.summary: This article lists new cars offering 0% financing in February 2026, highlighting the Kia EV9, Ram 1500, and Ineos Grenadier. It explains the benefits of 0% APR, stating that it can save buyers thousands of dollars, though not everyone with excellent credit will qualify. The article also provides a comprehensive table of models, brands, and terms for 0% financing offers, most of which expire on March 2, 2026.

feed.title: CSX's Advanced Locomotive Technology Reduces Fuel Consumption and Emissions
feed.summary: CSX has significantly reduced fuel consumption and emissions through enhancements to its locomotive technology, primarily with the Automatic Engine Stop-Start (AESS) system. An upgrade in 2024, the AESS Assist system, further improved shutdown performance and battery support, leading to increased fuel efficiency and extended battery life. Collaborating with Wabtec, CSX also implemented software upgrades to prolong engine shutdown times, reinforcing its commitment to lower operational costs and environmental sustainability.

feed.title: Multibillion-dollar chip deal aims to steady supply for smarter cars, factories
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries (GFS) and Renesas have announced an expanded multibillion-dollar manufacturing partnership to improve semiconductor supply resilience and accelerate U.S. chip manufacturing. The collaboration will broaden Renesas's access to GF's differentiated technologies, including FDX, BCD, and CMOS with non-volatile memory, to support various applications in automotive and industrial sectors. Manufacturing is set to begin in mid-2026 across GF's global footprint, with considerations for porting select GF processes into Renesas's Japan fabs.

feed.title: Buy This, Not That: 2026 Subaru Outback or 2026 Toyota 4Runner?
feed.summary: This article compares the 2026 Subaru Outback and 2026 Toyota 4Runner, two off-road-oriented SUVs catering to outdoor enthusiasts, to help potential buyers decide. It highlights their differences in design, interior space, powertrains, and pricing, noting the Outback's car-based practicality versus the 4Runner's truck-based ruggedness. The decision largely depends on whether the buyer prioritizes everyday comfort and affordability or superior off-road capability and towing.

feed.title: Maintenance will cause railroad crossing closures in Bryan
feed.summary: The City of Bryan announced that Union Pacific Railroad will be conducting maintenance projects, leading to several railroad crossing closures over the next two weeks. Work hours will be from 9 a.m. to 7 p.m. daily, starting February 17th. Message boards will provide advance notice and detour information, though dates are subject to change.

feed.title: China's BYD Just Beat Ford in Global Sales for the First Time Ever
feed.summary: In 2025, China's BYD Company surpassed Ford Motor Company in global vehicle sales for the first time, delivering approximately 4.6 million vehicles compared to Ford's 4.4 million. This achievement highlights BYD's focus on new energy vehicles (NEVs) and its significant growth in both domestic and international markets, now ranking as the sixth-largest global automaker. The shift underscores a broader transformation in the automotive industry as electric and plug-in hybrid vehicles gain market dominance.

feed.title: Energy Brief: Earnings, Rates, Solar, Global Energy Moves
feed.summary: This energy brief covers upcoming earnings reports from major energy companies, challenges to utility rate increases, and efforts to plug abandoned oil wells. It also highlights NextEra Energy's dividend increase and global energy developments, including Iran's nuclear agreement talks, agrivoltaics research, India's solar capacity surge, and the Philippines' green energy auction plans.

feed.title: First Trust Small Cap Value AlphaDEX Fund (NASDAQ:FYT) Short Interest Update
feed.summary: Short interest in First Trust Small Cap Value AlphaDEX Fund (NASDAQ:FYT) significantly decreased by 17.8% to 9,482 shares as of January 30th. This translates to 0.4% of shares outstanding and a days-to-cover ratio of 0.9. Several institutional investors increased their stakes, including Jones Financial Companies and Cetera Investment Advisers, while the fund recently raised its quarterly dividend.

feed.title: US Blackstone invests $600M in India’s AI company Neysa
feed.summary: US private equity firm Blackstone has led a primary equity investment of up to $600 million in India-based AI infrastructure startup Neysa. This investment will grant Blackstone a majority stake in Neysa, with co-investors also participating. The move aims to significantly boost India's domestic computing capacity, as the country seeks to develop its homegrown AI capabilities amidst rapidly increasing demand for local compute infrastructure.

feed.title: Apple Announces Unusual Multi-City “Apple Experience” for March 4
feed.summary: Apple has sent invitations for an "Apple Experience" on March 4, 2026, to be held simultaneously in New York, London, and Shanghai. This multi-city format suggests a different kind of announcement than a typical keynote, possibly a hands-on event for journalists. Speculation includes new MacBooks, iPhone 17e, MacBook Pro upgrades, or updated iPad Air and Apple TV models.

feed.title: Bank of America CEO Moynihan Sees GDP Outlook Improving, Consumer Spending Holding Up at BAC Conference
feed.summary: Bank of America CEO Brian Moynihan believes the U.S. economic outlook is improving, with the bank forecasting 2.8% GDP growth for 2026 and consumer spending up 5% year-over-year in January, supported by a strong labor market. Moynihan also outlined strategic targets for the bank, including 16%-18% return on tangible common equity (ROTCE) and 5%-7% net interest income growth, while discussing regulatory normalization and the bank's efforts to enhance consumer affordability.

feed.title: West Pharmaceutical Services launches Synchrony S1 prefillable syringe system
feed.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services has introduced its new Synchrony S1 prefillable syringe (PFS) system, designed to accelerate drug development. The system aims to benefit pharmaceutical applications and more information can be found in a recent publication by Drug Development & Delivery.

feed.title: Simon Property Group Q4 2025 Earnings Beat Estimates | 2026 Outlook - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Simon Property Group (SPG) reported Q4 2025 earnings that surpassed analyst expectations, with revenue of $1.8 billion and adjusted FFO per share of $3.49. The company projects full-year 2026 FFO between $13 and $13.25 per share. Despite underperforming the S&P 500 over the past year, the stock has accrued a 6.3% rise year-to-date in 2026, and analysts maintain a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $202.

feed.title: Armada Hoffler Properties, Inc. announces a change to its common stock trading symbol on the New York Stock Exchange
feed.summary: Armada Hoffler Properties, Inc. has announced a change in its common stock trading symbol on the New York Stock Exchange from "AHH" to "AHRT". This change affects only the trading code, with the company name remaining unchanged. The announcement was made by 老虎证券 on February 16, 2026, at 21:57.

feed.title: 5 Best Income Stocks in This Market
feed.summary: Despite concerns about hydrocarbons, midstream energy infrastructure offers durable income opportunities due to rising electricity demand from data centers, AI, and electrification. Midstream companies act as "toll collectors," generating predictable cash flow from moving and storing energy under long-term contracts. Five top stocks in this sector are Kinder Morgan, Energy Transfer, Plains All American, MPLX, and Enterprise Products Partners, with the InfraCap MLP ETF (AMZA) offering a simpler alternative for investors seeking diversification without K-1 tax forms.

feed.title: It’s Official: Five Below Is Now Open in Blackmore Marketplace
feed.summary: Five Below, a specialty discount store, has officially opened its doors in Casper's Blackmore Marketplace, having had its soft opening on Sunday, February 15th, 2026. This new store, which primarily offers items for $5 or less, is part of a larger wave of new retail establishments coming to the area. Five Below aims to attract teens and tweens but provides a diverse range of products appealing to all ages.

feed.title: Applied Materials Stock Jumps 8% After Q1 Earnings Beat and Strong Q2 Outlook
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) stock surged over 8% after reporting Q1 earnings that surpassed Wall Street expectations for revenue and adjusted EPS, along with providing strong Q2 guidance. The company's disciplined cost control and steady demand for semiconductor manufacturing tools contributed to its impressive performance. Despite trading at higher multiples post-rally, reflecting elevated expectations driven by AI, analysts currently maintain a modest price target above the current share price.

feed.title: CoStar Group Stock Performance 2025: CSGP Down 38.8%, Analyst Outlook - News and Statistics
feed.summary: CoStar Group (CSGP) stock has significantly underperformed the market over the past year, declining 38.8% while the S&P 500 rallied 11.8%. This underperformance followed disappointing Q3 2025 results and lower-than-expected forward guidance. Despite a negative outlook for 2025 EPS, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a mean price target suggesting a potential 78.5% upside.

feed.title: What Price Targets Have Wall Street Analysts Set for CoStar Group Shares?
feed.summary: CoStar Group (CSGP) has significantly underperformed the market, with its shares dropping 38.8% in the last year, despite consistently beating earnings estimates. Following cautious Q3 2025 guidance, analysts project a 9.3% decrease in EPS for fiscal year 2025. Wall Street maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus, with an average price target of $80.29, suggesting a potential 78.5% upside.

feed.title: Enphase Energy, Inc. (ENPH) Price Forecast: First Bullish Tests Breakout Support
feed.summary: Enphase Energy (ENPH) has confirmed a bullish trend reversal with a significant weekly breakout supported by record volume. Following a strong Q4 2025 earnings report and increased Q1 2026 guidance, the stock is now undergoing its first meaningful pullback since the reversal. Traders are watching key support levels at $42.63 and the 20-day moving average ($41.97) to see if renewed strength emerges for a potential new upward leg.

feed.title: Inspiring Curiosity, Energizing the Future: NiSource Charitable Foundation Hosts Annual ‘Introduce a Girl to STEAM’ Event Across Five States
feed.summary: The NiSource Charitable Foundation, in partnership with Columbia Gas and NIPSCO, hosted its second annual ‘Introduce a Girl to STEAM Signature Event’ across five states, engaging over 300 school-aged girls. This initiative aims to inspire young women to pursue careers in Science, Technology, Engineering, Art, and Mathematics (STEAM) by providing hands-on learning experiences and connecting them with women leaders in these fields. The program supports the Foundation's mission to foster strong communities and develop future innovators.

feed.title: Brown and Brown Publishes 2026 Market Trends Report
feed.summary: Brown & Brown, Inc. has released its 2026 Market Trends report, offering a comprehensive analysis of the evolving insurance landscape. The report, which is the first since the acquisition of Risk Strategies, suggests that rate conditions are improving for well-prepared buyers with positive claims histories, while certain industries and product lines will continue to face challenging terms. It highlights unique challenges across commercial, employee benefits, and personal insurance segments, emphasizing the importance of strong risk management practices and digital health tools to navigate market dynamics.

feed.title: VantageScore Vs. FICO: Understanding The Key Differences For Your Mortgage
feed.summary: This article explains the key differences between VantageScore and FICO, the two primary credit scoring models used by lenders, especially after recent policy changes allowing VantageScore 4.0 for conventional loans. It details how each score is calculated, their respective ranges, and specific factors that impact them, highlighting how VantageScore's more inclusive approach could open up homeownership opportunities for millions. The piece also advises consumers on which score to monitor based on their financial goals and how to check their credit scores and reports.

feed.title: UBS Maintains Neutral on ES Eversource Energy Feb 16 2026, PT $80
feed.summary: UBS has reiterated its Neutral rating on Eversource Energy (ES) but raised its price target from $75 to $80 on February 16, 2026. This adjustment reflects modest optimism following Eversource's Q4 2025 results and a $26.5 billion capital plan aimed at 5-7% EPS growth through 2028. The maintained Neutral rating suggests UBS sees balanced upside and downside potential for the utility, advising investors to assess their position size based on income needs and regulatory risks.

feed.title: Elon Musk, Tim Cook add voices to chorus of warnings about ‘unprecedented’ chip crisis
feed.summary: Tech industry leaders like Elon Musk and Tim Cook are sounding alarms about an "unprecedented" global memory chip shortage. This crunch, driven by the booming demand for AI data centers and the production of HBM (High-Bandwidth Memory) for AI accelerators, is expected to inflate prices and constrain production across various sectors, from smartphones to automobiles. Manufacturers like Sony and Chinese smartphone makers are already seeing disruptions, with analysts predicting the imbalance between supply and demand to persist for years.

feed.title: New Jersey American Water Announces Launch of 2026 American Water Charitable Foundation Water and Environment Grant Program – Company Announcement - FT.com
feed.summary: New Jersey American Water has launched the 2026 American Water Charitable Foundation Water and Environment Grant Program, inviting community partners to apply for grants supporting clean water, conservation, environmental education, climate variability, and water-based recreation projects. In 2025, the Foundation awarded $1.7 million to 80 organizations across 12 states. The program aims to uphold the Foundation's commitment to Water, People, and Communities, having invested over $25 million in grants since 2012.

feed.title: International Flavors & Fragrances (IFF) Is Up 10.4% After 2026 Guidance And Food Ingredients Sale Plan
feed.summary: International Flavors & Fragrances (IFF) stock rose 10.4% following its Q4 2025 report, which included mixed results, 2026 guidance, and a plan to sell its Food Ingredients business. The company aims to reinvest proceeds into higher-margin segments like Taste, Scent, and Health & Biosciences, focusing on cash flow and balance sheet strength. This divestiture is seen as a move to simplify the portfolio and support margin expansion, though it carries execution risks related to lost scale and stranded costs.

feed.title: Should You Buy Live Nation Entertainment Stock Before Feb. 19?
feed.summary: Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) is set to report Q4 earnings on Feb. 19, with the stock underperforming the broader market over the past year. While stadium shows drove strong Q3 profits and 2026 ticket sales are robust, investors face a dilemma due to the stock's high P/E ratio of 115 and a history of negative earnings surprises. Additionally, ongoing regulatory scrutiny and antitrust trials introduce further uncertainty.

feed.title: City Office REIT Announces Dividends for Second Quarter 2024
feed.summary: City Office REIT, Inc. (NYSE: CIO) announced that its Board of Directors has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.10 per share for common stock and common partnership units, and $0.4140625 per share for its 6.625% Series A Cumulative Redeemable Preferred Stock. These dividends are payable on July 24, 2024, to stockholders of record as of July 10, 2024. City Office REIT specializes in acquiring and operating high-quality office properties in Sun Belt markets.

feed.title: Avient to pay shareholders 27.5 cents per share in April
feed.summary: Avient (NYSE: AVNT) has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.275 per share. This dividend will be paid on April 3, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 17, 2026. The company, an innovator in materials solutions, emphasizes its commitment to sustainability and customer success through a global presence and diverse product portfolio.

feed.title: Graphic Packaging to Present at Raymond James Institutional Investors Conference on March 2
feed.summary: Graphic Packaging Holding Company announced that its President and CEO, Robbert Rietbroek, will present at the Raymond James Institutional Investors Conference on March 2nd at 11:35 AM ET. The presentation will be available live and in replay via webcast on the company's investor relations website. Graphic Packaging is a global leader in sustainable consumer packaging, producing items primarily from renewable or recycled materials.

feed.title: Google stock price: Alphabet’s GOOG faces a Tuesday test after Presidents Day pause
feed.summary: Alphabet's Class C shares (GOOG) closed at $306.02, down 1.1%, before the Presidents Day holiday, and investors are now focused on Tuesday's market reopening. The stock is facing renewed scrutiny regarding its substantial AI investments and the speed at which these investments will yield returns. Concerns about high capital expenditures and increasing debt, amid a busy week for U.S. economic data and upcoming earnings from Nvidia, are contributing to investor caution.

feed.title: Dollar Tree makes an upscale play to fuel sales
feed.summary: Dollar Tree is strategically opening new stores in more affluent areas to attract higher-income customers who spend more per visit. This shift has resulted in 60% of new Dollar Tree customers making at least six figures, driving potential annual sales increases. The company aims to boost higher-income shoppers' visit frequency, acknowledging that current inflation trends are also leading more households to seek out discount retailers.

feed.title: Garmin Opens Aviation Facility At Mesa Gateway Airport
feed.summary: Garmin has opened a new aviation facility at Mesa Gateway Airport in Mesa, Arizona, acquiring a hangar and office complex to expand its capacity for aircraft certification and flight test operations. This new location, which includes two hangars totaling 75,000 square feet, will accommodate approximately 75 associates and supports Garmin's continued investment in its aviation segment. The Mesa facility joins existing flight operations centers and leverages the strategic advantages of the Mesa Gateway Airport, including robust infrastructure and favorable weather conditions.

feed.title: Bad news for skyrocketing ticket prices: Live Nation could catch break from Trump admin
feed.summary: A report from Semafor indicates that Live Nation may avoid a major antitrust trial with the federal government through a settlement negotiated with the Trump administration officials, despite objections from the Justice Department's Antitrust Division. This potential settlement, amidst growing tensions within the DOJ and the ouster of the antitrust chief, is seen by independent promoters and consumer advocates as a significant setback for competition and consumer affordability in the live music industry. Critics argue it signals a triumph of political influence and lobbying over legal enforcement, while state attorneys general continue their own fight against Live Nation's alleged monopolistic practices.

feed.title: Global Payments Reshapes Core Business As Analysts Reassess Undervalued Shares
feed.summary: Global Payments (NYSE:GPN) is undergoing a significant strategic shift, divesting non-core business units and refreshing its technology platforms to enhance its core payments operations. While the company's shares have seen recent declines, analysts are reassessing its value, with Simply Wall St identifying the stock as undervalued despite short-term momentum issues. The success of this reshaping will hinge on effective execution and its ability to improve the company's competitive standing and investor perception.

feed.title: Armada Hoffler Unveils Bold New Strategic Direction to Drive Long-Term Shareholder Value and Launches as AH Realty Trust
feed.summary: Armada Hoffler (NYSE: AHH) announced a major business restructuring, renaming itself AH Realty Trust (NYSE: AHRT) effective March 2, 2026. This transformation involves exiting the multifamily property sector, divesting its construction and real estate financing businesses, and focusing on retail and office properties to reduce leverage and drive long-term shareholder value. The company aims for a leaner, more disciplined operating model, with proceeds from asset sales directed towards debt reduction and a new executive compensation structure aligning with shareholder returns.

feed.title: Real estate trust exits multifamily, rebrands as AH Realty Trust
feed.summary: Armada Hoffler (NYSE: AHH) is rebranding to AH Realty Trust (AHRT) effective March 2, 2026, and is undertaking a major strategic shift by exiting the multifamily sector, divesting its construction and financing platforms, and refocusing on retail and office properties. The company plans to sell 11 of its 14 multifamily assets, using the proceeds for debt reduction to achieve a target of 5.5x–6.5x net debt/adjusted EBITDA. This transformation also includes leadership changes, a rightsized dividend, and a modernized executive compensation program aimed at long-term shareholder value creation.

feed.title: Xcel Energy planning outages for Texas Panhandle amidst heightened fire risk
feed.summary: Xcel Energy is planning Public Safety Power Shutoffs for approximately 15,000 customers in the Texas Panhandle starting Tuesday, February 17, at 9 a.m. The outages are a precautionary measure due to heightened fire risk caused by very high winds, low humidity, and dry fuels. Customers in affected counties are advised to prepare with outage kits and backup power for essential medical equipment, as power restoration could take several hours to several days.

feed.title: Apple Announces a Special Launch Event. Here’s What to Expect
feed.summary: Apple is set to host a special, invite-only launch event on March 4 in New York, London, and Shanghai, diverging from its usual Cupertino campus launches. The event is expected to unveil several new products, including the $599 iPhone 17e, a new budget-friendly MacBook starting at $699, and updated iPad models. High-end MacBook Pro and MacBook Air models will also receive internal upgrades with more powerful M5 chips.
